author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

git-bug stores all data in `refs/bugs/<id>` — one ref per
bug, each ref's commit chain is that bug's op log. Each
commit's tree carries the current snapshot (issue JSON,
comments) and the commit message carries the op trailer.
Standard `git push refs/bugs/*:refs/bugs/*` round-trips
cleanly via every git host (Forgejo / GitHub / GitLab /
Gitea); no server-side config needed. Concurrent writes
are handled by a DAG-of-ops merge — divergence creates a
merge commit with an empty ops array, and replay is
deterministic by Lamport clock. The 34 archived
`refs/bugs/*` refs in this repo confirm the shape
(empty-blob metadata files, `ops` blob as JSON array,
clock-as-filename). Web UIs only show `refs/heads/` and
`refs/tags/`, so issue refs are invisible from the web
view — same as git-bug's experience.

jjforge's current trailer format (`Jjf-Op:` stanzas), op
vocabulary, and boundary validation (`validate_title`,
`validate_no_newlines`, self-dep check, phantom-target
check, concurrent-write retry from the recent QA round)
are all portable as-is to a per-issue-ref layout.

# Recommendation

Per-issue refs with commit-chain-of-op-packs (git-bug's
shape). Refs at `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` and
`refs/jjf/memories/<key>`; sync via `refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*`.
Design doc landed at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` —
covers storage shape, op log shape, write path, read
path, sync refspec, v2 → v3 migration, what stays vs what
goes away, cost estimate.

Implementation epic filed as `bd98097` with 8 sketched
child tickets (write path, read path, init, migrator,
push/pull, snapshot cache, test sweep, guard removal).
Serial dispatch order: 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5 → 6 → 7 → 8.
Tests green between each. Estimated 3–4 sessions of
focused work.

On the blocked asterinas migration: recommend WAITING
for v3 rather than shipping the sibling-clone interim.
The migration is large (~968 issues) and the sibling-
clone workaround would persist on every read/write of
asterinas data forever until v3 — wrong layer to pay
that cost. Asterinas markdown tree was an archive long
before this and can stay one for 3–4 sessions. The
`blocks` edge on `cc2fa96` stays; `bd98097` blocks
`cc2fa96`.

# Confidence

High on the storage shape — git-bug's pattern is
production-proven for years, the design maps cleanly
onto jjforge's existing op/trailer machinery, and the
34 archived refs in this very repo are a working
reference. High on the cost estimate — every piece of
the work is a known rewrite of an existing module, no
greenfield design.

Medium on the v2 → v3 migrator's edge cases: what
happens to in-flight remote-tracking state when a user
migrates a repo that's currently pulled-but-not-pushed?
That falls out in the migrator child ticket (epic
child #4); not a blocker on the design.

# Open follow-ups

- File child tickets under `bd98097` as discrete
  feature tickets (1 per child) when starting the
  implementation epic. The sketched list lives in the
  epic body; concretize them just-in-time.
- After v3 ships, retire `JJF_ALLOW_SELF_HOST` from
  CLAUDE.md and close `08cf14b`.
- After v3 ships, unblock `cc2fa96` and re-attempt the
  asterinas migration via the Haiku fan-out per
  `docs/host-asterinas-audit.md`.
